gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/245)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/245)
text: حَجَّ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ بِالنَّاسِ سَنَةَ أَرْبَعِينَ وَ مِائَةٍ فَسَقَطَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ بَغْلَتِهِ فَوَقَفَ عَلَيْهِ إِسْمَاعِيلُ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) سِرْ فَإِنَّ الْإِمَامَ لَا يَقِفُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Mansour Bin Al Abbas, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Bin Yaqteen, from Hafs Al Mowzan who said,

‘Ismail Bin Ali led the people to Hajj in the year one hundred and forty, so Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> fell off from his<sup>asws</sup> mule. So Ismail paused over him<sup>asws</sup>, but Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said to him: ‘Keep going, for the leader does not pause’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Mansur ibn al-‘Abbas from al-Hassan ibn Ali ibn Yaqtin from Hafs al-Mu’adhdhin who has said the following: “Isma‘il ibn Ali (appointed leader for pilgrim) performed al-Hajj in the year 140 Hijriy. Abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, fell off his transporting mule and Isma‘il ibn Ali stopped to care for him. Abu ‘Abd Allah, Alayhi al-Salam, said, ‘Move along with people; the (Imam) leader must not stop.’”
